titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 81

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=126 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:28:01.700552+00 | translation moa=127 status=live modified=2016-06-07 15:11:42.774104+00

div.p:
Vniuersis sancte matris ecclesie filiis Ricardus dei gratia duncheldensis episcopus salutem ⁊ episcopalem
benedictionem . Quoniam ad nostrum spectat officium sancte religionis cultum amplificare 
noscant tam posteri quam presentes me concessisse & hac mea carta confirmasse abbati de
dunfermel’ ⁊ monachis ibidem deo seruientibus donationem regis malcholmi ⁊ an
dree episcopi katenensis secundum quod eorum carte testantur ecclesiam sancte ∙ trinitatis de dunchelde
⁊ omnes terras iuste ad eam pertinentes liberas ⁊ quietas ab omni exactione \tam/ ecclesiastice quam secularis
persone saluo episcopali iure . Concedo etiam eis conuersationem in episcopatu meo . ⁊ ut di
uinum exerceant officium ⁊ subditorum suorum curam habeant animarum ⁊ ut conuersan
tes in diocesi mea a me quod ad christianitatem pertinerit  accipiant . Testibus . Matheo archidi
acono . \sancti . Andree/ Bricio priore de insula Michaele clerico . Magistro matheo . & Iohanne fra
tre suo . Roberto dapifero episcopi Radulfo capellano . Thoma presbitero . Murdac cle
rico . Abraham paruo .

p.cl: To all sons of Holy Mother Church Richard, by the grace of God bishop of Dunkeld, sends greeting and episcopal blessing.
Since to augment the worship of holy religion belongs to our office,
let those to come as well as those present know

that I have granted and by this my charter made firm, to the abbot of Dunfermline and to the monks serving God in that place, the donation of King Malcolm and of Andrew bishop of Caithness, according to that which is attested in their charters: the church of the Holy Trinity at Dunkeld and all the lands rightfully belonging to it, free and quit from all exaction of the ecclesiastical as well as of the secular person, saving episcopal right. I also allow them their mode of living in my bishopric, and that they may perform their divine office and have the cure of souls of those under their authority; and that, living in my diocese, they may receive from me that which concerns Christianity.

As witnesses: Matthew, archdeacon of St Andrews; Brice, prior of Inchcolm; Michael the clerk; Master Matthew; and John, his brother; Robert the bishop’s steward; Ralph the chaplain; Thomas the presbyter; Murdoch the clerk; Abraham Small.
